channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate passes Social Security benefits boost for many public service retirees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed legislation early Saturday to boost Social Security payments for millions of people, pushing a longtime priority for former public employees through Congress in one of its last acts for the year.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate sends spending deal to Biden's desk, averting shutdown
item.description: The Senate in the early hours of Saturday passed a stopgap funding package, avoiding a government shutdown that would have furloughed hundreds of thousands of federal workers and bringing a tumultuous week in Congress to a close.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KTLA 5

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Facing a government shutdown deadline, the Senate rushed through final passage early Saturday of a bipartisan plan that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, dropping President-elect Donald Trump's demands for a debt limit increase into the new year.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sectarian violence in Syria has been less intense than feared since Assad's ouster
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The toppling of Bashar Assad has raised tentative hopes that Syrians might live peacefully and as equals after a half century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Indonesians mark 2 decades since the tragic tsunami that killed hundreds of thousands
item.description: BANDA ACEH, Indonesia (AP) — Two decades after a catastrophic tsunami destroyed her village, Tria Asnani still cries when she recalls how she lost her mother while trying to escape the giant waves. Asnani, now a school teacher, was only 17 at the time.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father accused of beheading son in Sacramento County
item.description: (FOX40.COM) -- Sacramento County deputies arrested a 28-year-old man on Friday morning in connection with the death of his 1-year-old son. Andrey Demskiy, 28, is accused of beheading his son.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri governor commutes sentence of white police officer convicted of fatally shooting Black man
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A white former Kansas City police officer who was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in the fatal shooting of a Black man was released from prison Friday after Missouri’s governor commuted his sentence to parole. The decision by Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baby pigs get Christmas pardon from Florida mayor in a Cuban twist on White House turkey tradition
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — As the stars of Friday's show in downtown Miami, baby pigs Glinda and Elphaba will never have to worry about gracing the dinner table at a Cuban American family's home on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old girl at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Obama reveals his favorite books of 2024
item.description: With 2024 coming to a close, former President Obama is again sharing out a list of his favorites, posting his ten favorite books of the year.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, KRON4

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio lawmakers approve charging up to $750 for police and jail videos
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — People seeking copies of police and jail videos in Ohio may have to pay up to $75 for each hour if Gov. Mike DeWine signs a measure approved by the state legislature this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delphi murders: Richard Allen sentenced for killing 2 teen girls on hiking trail
item.description: Richard Allen, the Indiana man convicted of killing two girls along a Delphi hiking trail in 2017, infamously known as the Delphi murders, was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Talk to Santa on ChatGPT and other festive tech hacks
item.description: Santa has arrived on ChatGPT and you can talk to him! You can access the new (and limited!) Santa Chat feature through the ChatGPT app by starting a new chat, hitting the live chat button and then using the setting switch to choose a voice.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Strange Connecticut laws, such as receiving a $99 fine for selling silly string to a minor
item.description: Among the keywords you can find in Connecticut law include "silly string," "balloons" and "arcade games." All these topics are involved in some of the state's strangest laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks workers begin strike that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at some Starbucks stores on Friday began a five-day strike to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FAA temporarily restricts drone flights in New York amid concerns over recently reported sightings
item.description: The FAA issued temporary restrictions on drone flights in 27 areas of New York in response to the influx of reported sightings in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
